content.full_text:
FAIR p OLITICAL p RACTICES COMMISSION 
428 J Street • Suite 620 • Sacramento, CA 95814-2329 
(916) 322-5660 • Fax (916) 322-0886 
July 27, 2011 
Phillip A. Passafuime 
Dawson, Passafuime, Bowden & Martinez 
4665 Scotts Valley Drive 
Scotts Valley, California 95066 
RE: Your Request for Informal Advice 
Our File No. 1-11-116 
Dear Mr. Passafuime: 
This letter responds to your request for advice on behalf of Scotts Valley Mayor 
Dene Bustichi regarding the conflict-of-interest provisions of the Political Reform Act (the 
"Act").1 This letter is based on the facts presented; the Fair Political Practices Commission (the 
"Commission") does not act as a finder of fact when it renders assistance. (In re Oglesby (1975) 
1 FPPC Ops. 71.) We are unable to provide assistance with respect to any conduct that has 
already taken place. Please bear in mind that nothing in this letter should be construed as 
evaluation of any past conduct. Because one of your questions is general in nature, we are 
treating your request as one for informal assistance.2 
Additionally, our advice is limited to obligations arising under the Act. We do not 
address the applicability, if any, of other conflict-of-interest laws such as common-law conflict 
of interest or Government Code Section 1090. 
QUESTIONS 
1. May Mayor Bustichi participate in the approval of a development if the Mayor's 
construction business plans to bid on the project? What if his firm does not plan to bid? 
1 The Political Reform Act is contained in Government Code Sections 81000 through 91014. All statutory 
references are to the Government Code, unless otherwise indicated. The regulations of the Fair Political Practices 
Commission are contained in Sections 18110 through 18997 of Title 2 of the California Code of Regulations. All 
regulatory references are to Title 2, Division 6 of the California Code of Regulations, unless otherwise indicated. 
2 Informal assistance does not provide the requestor with the immunity provided by an opinion or formal 
written advice. (Section 83114; Regulation 18329( c)(3 ).) 


File No. 1-11-116 
Page No. 2 
2. In the future if Mayor Bustichi abstains from participating in a vote to approve a 
development project, would his construction business be prohibited from bidding on the 
development project? 
CONCLUSIONS 
1. Because Mayor Bustichi has already participated in the governmental decision to 
approve the building project, that action is past conduct and we are unable to advice on that 
governmental decision. 
2. The Act does not apply to private decisions of public officials such as whether or not to 
bid on a project. The Act only prohibits the Mayor from making, participating in making or 
attempting to use his official position to influence a governmental decision in which he has an 
economic interest. If his business were going to bid on a development, he wottld not be able to 
make, participate in making or attempt to use his official position to influence the decision. 
FACTS 
You are the authorized representative for the Mayor of Scotts Valley, Dene Bustichi. 
Mayor Bustichi is the owner and President of BCI Builder's, Inc. ("BCI"). The business is 
located within the city limits of Scotts Valley. BCI is engaged in construction of residential and 
commercial properties and public works projects. As of two years ago BCI was engaged almost 
exclusively in the building of public projects. During that time, Mayor Bustichi was an active 
member of the City Council and participated in a unanimous vote of the Council approving a 
development that BCI now intends to bid on. Subsequent to the initial approval, Mayor Bustichi 
has disqualified himself from voting on any issue regarding the development. Mayor Bustichi 
would like to know if his previous vote to approve the development now disqualifies his 
business, BCI from bidding on the development project. He would also like to know if BCI 
would be prohibited from bidding on building future projects before the City Council if he 
disqualifies himself from voting on issues related to future developments. 
ANALYSIS 
POTENTIAL CONFLICT OF INTEREST 
The Act's conflict-of-interest provisions ensure that public officials will "perform their 
duties in an impartial manner, free from bias caused by their own financial interests or the 
financial interests of persons who have supported them." (Section 81001(b).) Section 87100 
prohibits any public official from making, participating in making, or otherwise using his or her 
official position to influence a governmental decision in which the official has a financial 
interest. 
' 
The Commission has adopted an eight-step standard analysis for deciding whether an 
official has a disqualifying conflict of interest. (Regulation 18700(b).) The general rule, 
however, is that a conflict of interest exists whenever a public official makes a governmental 


File No. I-11-116 
Page No. 3 
decision that has a reasonably foreseeable material financial effect on one or more of his or her 
financial interests. 
As a member of the Scotts Valley City Council, Mayor Bustichi is a public official under 
the Act. (Section 82048.) Consequently, he may not make, participate in making, or otherwise use 
his official position to influence any decision that will have a reasonably foreseeable material 
financial effect on any of his economic interests. Mayor Bustichi has already participated in the 
vote to approve the development project that he would now like to have his business, BCI bid 
upon. However, because you have asked about past conduct we are unable to advice on that 
matter. 
You have indicated that there may be future governmental decisions before the City 
Council involving other development projects. Should Mayor Bustichi abstain from a future 
governmental decision involving another development project, he would not be participating in a 
governmental decision and would not have a conflict of interest in the governmental decision, 
irrespective of whether his firm intended to bid on the project or not. 
If you have other questions on this matter, please contact me at (916) 322-5660. 
SKB:jgl 
Sincerely, 
Zackery P. Morazzini 
General Counsel 
By: 
Sukhi K. Brar 
Commission Counsel, Legal Division
